query: ECB supervisory publications scrapped simplification push number of publications dropped 2025 banks clearer expectations

mako.raw:
{"query":"ECB supervisory publications scrapped simplification push number of publications dropped 2025 banks clearer expectations","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1983,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.bankingsupervision.europa.eu/press/pr/date/2026/html/ssm.pr260626~57c6ccd373.en.html","title":"ECB streamlines supervisory guidance to improve clarity and transparency","content":"The European Central Bank (ECB) is conducting a comprehensive review of its publications on banking supervision to improve transparency, consistency and ease of use for banks and other stakeholders, including the wider public.
The review supports these objectives by streamlining guidance, clarifying the purpose of different supervisory tools and ensuring clear, consistent and accessible communication.
Such publications are an essential tool for European banking supervision.
They explain how the ECB carries out its supervisory tasks and communicate supervisory expectations and good practices in a non‑binding manner.
A list of these publications is provided below.
A limited number of publications have been (or will shortly be) revised, for example to clarify specific aspects or reflect recent regulatory developments.
All content related to supervisory expectations on the credit conversion factor has been removed from the Guide to internal models, since the European Banking Authority (EBA) is in the process of preparing guidelines on this topic.
Some publications will be subject to more in-depth revisions, to take full account of forthcoming legislative developments and feedback on publications received from banks and other stakeholders.
Publication of the updated guide is expected in the third quarter of 2026."},{"url":"https://www.centralbanking.com/central-banks/financial-stability/micro-prudential/7976239/ecb-scraps-40-supervisory-publications-in-simplification-push","title":"ECB scraps 40 supervisory publications in simplification push - Central Banking","content":"ECB scraps 40 supervisory publications in simplification push - Central Banking Skip to main content
The European Central Bank has completed a review of its supervisory guides and discontinued 30% of them amid its push for simplification.
In a press release today (June 26), the ECB said it had assessed all supervisory guides, reports, letters and methodologies available on its website, totalling around 130 documents.
The bank said it had identified around 40 documents that were “outdated, superseded or no longer relevant”, and which it had therefore discontinued."},{"url":"https://sg.finance.yahoo.com/news/ecb-scrap-40-supervisory-publications-234647671.html","title":"ECB to scrap 40 supervisory publications to banks to cut burden","content":"The ECB reviewed more than 130 such documents "to make them more concise and user-friendly," Frank Elderson, a member of the ECB's executive board, wrote in a blog post on Friday.
(June 26): The European Central Bank (ECB) will discontinue about 40 of its regulatory guides to lenders, part of efforts to reduce the burden of its oversight of the industry."},{"url":"https://www.irishexaminer.com/business/economy/arid-41869596.html","title":"ECB softens governance expectations on banks","content":"The European Central Bank will retire a number of recommendations and guides for banks and soften its expectations about good governance, it said on Friday, partly in response to industry criticism.
As part of this effort, the ECB said it would scrap around 40 supervisory publications that it deems outdated.
In addition, it would downgrade a draft guide setting out its expectations for lenders' governance and risk culture, which covered the inner workings of a bank from the remuneration and time-commitment of its board members to the protection of whistleblowers."},{"url":"https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-26/ecb-to-scrap-40-supervisory-publications-to-banks-to-cut-burden","title":"ECB to Scrap 40 Supervisory Publications to Banks to Cut ...","content":"Jun 26, 2026 — ECB to Scrap 40 Supervisory Publications to Banks to Cut Burden Comfort June 26, 2026. ECB reviewed more than 130 such documents “to make them ..."}]}

The European Central Bank (ECB) is conducting a comprehensive review of its publications on banking supervision to improve transparency, consistency and ease of use for banks and other stakeholders, including the wider public. The review is based on a comprehensive inventory of key publications available on the ECB’s banking supervision website expressing supervisory expectations and setting good practices. * Supervisory expectations for the management buffer will be clarified in the Guide to the internal capital adequacy assessment process. Finally, as part of this work, ECB Banking Supervision has updated the classification of supervisory publications on its website to clarify the purpose and intended use of different publication types, including supervisory guides, reports, letters and methodologies, and thereby ensure consistency across publications and alignment with the EU legal framework.","score":0.8259413,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.centralbanking.com/central-banks/financial-stability/micro-prudential/7976239/ecb-scraps-40-supervisory-publications-in-simplification-push","title":"ECB scraps 40 supervisory publications in simplification push","content":"The European Central Bank has completed a review of its supervisory guides and discontinued 30% of them amid its push for simplification.","score":0.7997713,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.pymnts.com/legal/bank-regulation/2026/ecb-scraps-40-outdated-supervisory-guides-to-boost-european-bank-lending","title":"ECB Scraps 40 Outdated Supervisory Guides to Boost European Bank ...","content":"The European Central Bank (ECB) has discontinued about 40 supervisory publications and has revised others in an effort to provide simpler","score":0.7763013,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/legal/legalindustry/ecb-cuts-banks-reporting-duties-softens-governance-expectations-2026-06-26","title":"ECB softens governance expectations for banks to ease burden","content":"the ECB said it would scrap around 40 supervisory publications that it deems outdated.
